strategies.name: Direct indefinite integration
strategies.rationale: Integrate the slope function directly to get the general antiderivative, then solve for the constant using the given point.
steps_outline: Recognize that dy/dx = 2x^2 - 5, so y = integral of (2x^2 - 5) dx
Apply basic power rule: integral of 2x^2 dx = 2x^3/3, integral of -5 dx = -5x

strategies.name: Definite integral from initial point
strategies.rationale: Use the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus to express y(x) as y(3) plus the definite integral of the slope from 3 to x, avoiding the constant-finding step.
steps_outline: By FTC: y(x) = y(3) + integral from 3 to x of (2t^2 - 5) dt
Substitute y(3) = 2: y(x) = 2 + integral from 3 to x of (2t^2 - 5) dt

strategies.name: Separable differential equation
strategies.rationale: Treat dy/dx = f(x) as a separable ODE, separate variables, integrate both sides, and apply the initial condition.
steps_outline: Integrate both sides: integral dy = integral (2x^2 - 5) dx
Left side gives y, right side gives (2/3)x^3 - 5x + C
